title: Views and grids
text: board stores structure geometry two aligned slots, keyed view id — read board_views() board_grids(). kept apart purpose: membership owned update lifecycle (add / remove block), geometry client (drag sash, board records ). panel placed view slots; two reconciled placement read. third representation know name rarely touch: dock_layout. dockView’s form — {grid, panels, activeGroup} payload front end renders echoes back. wire type client boundary, something author . model dock_view + dock_grid; dock_layout crosses dockView (?dock_layout). constructor forgiving slot fill: layout real geometry usually just pass grids; views come along free. Reach views want display name differs id, view without explicit arrangement.

title: Views and grids
text: describe geometry dock_grid() (helpers panels() group()). Two rules cover everything: List nesting alternates orientation. dock_grid()’s top level lays children horizontally; one level list() nesting flips vertical; another flips back, . Character vectors create tabs. vector IDs shares one DockView panel tabs; list() IDs split separate panels.

title: Views and grids
text: single ID fills whole view: view named grid’s list name (Page), members grid’s panels ().

title: Views and grids
text: Two top-level children → two columns split horizontally:

title: Views and grids
text: Wrap children one extra layer list() introduce vertical split: outer level still describes horizontal split, single child “split” one full-width column. inner list("", "b") depth 1, splits vertically: stacks top b.

title: Views and grids
text: Use character vector (list) put several panels one DockView panel tabs: dock_grid(list("", "b")) (panels split) dock_grid(c("", "b")) (panels tabbed) read almost produce different UIs — list/vector distinction flips split panel tabify panel.

title: Views and grids
text: Combine two rules build arrangement.

what: Two columns, the right one stacked
title: Views and grids
Views and grids
text: third level nesting flips back horizontal inside vertical stack — row holding two panels side side:

title: Views and grids
text: common dashboard shape: narrow column left holding extension, tabbed panel right several blocks: also shape new_dock_board() gives default blocks extensions present, pass neither slot: extensions left, blocks right.
code: new_dock_board(   blocks = c(a = new_dataset_block(), b = new_head_block()),   extensions = new_edit_board_extension(),   grids = list(     Page = dock_grid("edit_board", c("a", "b"))   ) ) ┌────────────────────────────┐ │  Page  [+]                 │ ├─────────┬──────────────────┤ │         │ ┌────┐┌────┐     │ │  edit   │ │ a  ││ b  │     │ │         │ └────┘└────┘     │ │         │                  │ │         │   (tabs)         │ └─────────┴──────────────────┘

title: Views and grids
text: Give grids one entry; list name becomes separate page view-nav dropdown, becomes view: Analysis view (active default — first view wins): Switching Editor via dropdown: grid follows exactly syntax single-view form: character vectors tabs, nested lists splits.

what: Display names distinct from the id
title: Views and grids
text: grid’s list name view id, doubles display label. want label differs id — view geometry — fill views slot . dock_view() carries membership display name; align grid id: nav shows Analysis; stable id main active, renames updates address.
code: new_dock_board(   blocks = c(a = new_dataset_block(), b = new_head_block()),   views = list(     main = dock_view(c("a", "b"), name = "Analysis")   ),   grids = list(     main = dock_grid("a", "b")   ) )

title: Views and grids
text: first view active default. start elsewhere, name new_dock_board(active = ), using view id: view active single field views collection, keyed id — belongs collection, one view, always exactly one. Change runtime active_view(board) <- id, read back active_view(board); index board_grids(board)[[id]] need view’s geometry.
code: new_dock_board(   blocks = c(a = new_dataset_block(), b = new_head_block()),   extensions = new_edit_board_extension(),   grids = list(     Analysis = dock_grid("a", "b"),     Editor = dock_grid("edit_board")   ),   active = "Editor" ) ┌────────────────────────────┐ │ Editor  [+]   ← starts here│ ├────────────────────────────┤ │           edit             │ └────────────────────────────┘

title: Views and grids
text: Pass views alone (bare member vectors coerce dock_views) structure explicit arrangement. view renders flat default members client arranges (board records): empty view shows watermark prompt empty board, scoped tab.
code: new_dock_board(   blocks = c(a = new_dataset_block(), b = new_head_block()),   views = list(     Analysis = c("a", "b"),     Empty = character()   ) )

title: Views and grids
text: pot-pourri: multiple views, nested grids, tabbed panels, extension sidebar, explicit active view. Three views; Charts active, user lands first. Charts (active load): one tabbed panel, plot1 shown, plot2 tab. Data: slim extension sidebar left, wide right column raw / cleaned tabbed. active = "cleaned" opens cleaned tab; sizes = c(0.25, 0.75) carves narrow sidebar. Analysis: two columns 55/45. left nested group() 40/60 vertical split (summary plot1); right plot2.
code: new_dock_board(   blocks = c(     raw = new_dataset_block(),     cleaned = new_head_block(),     summary = new_head_block(),     plot1 = new_scatter_block(),     plot2 = new_scatter_block()   ),   extensions = new_edit_board_extension(),   links = list(     new_link("raw", "cleaned", "data"),     new_link("cleaned", "summary", "data"),     new_link("cleaned", "plot1", "data"),     new_link("cleaned", "plot2", "data")   ),   grids = list(     Data = dock_grid(       "edit_board",       panels("raw", "cleaned", active = "cleaned"),       sizes = c(0.25, 0.75)     ),     Analysis = dock_grid(       group("summary", "plot1", sizes = c(0.4, 0.6)),       "plot2",       sizes = c(0.55, 0.45)     ),     Charts = dock_grid(panels("plot1", "plot2"))   ),   active = "Charts" ) ┌────────────────────────────┐ │  Charts  [+]               │ ├────────────────────────────┤ │ ┌───────┐┌───────┐         │ │ │ plot1 ││ plot2 │         │ │ └───────┘└───────┘         │ │  (plot1 shown, plot2 tab)  │ └────────────────────────────┘ ┌────────────────────────────┐ │  Data  [+]                 │ ├──────┬─────────────────────┤ │      │┌─────┐┌─────────┐   │ │ edit ││ raw ││ cleaned │   │ │      │└─────┘└─────────┘   │ │      │  (cleaned shown)    │ └──────┴─────────────────────┘   25%          75% ┌────────────────────────────┐ │  Analysis  [+]             │ ├─────────────┬──────────────┤ │   summary   │              │   40% / 55% ├─────────────┤    plot2     │ │    plot1    │              │   60% / 45% └─────────────┴──────────────┘      55%             45%

what: Custom split ratios and active tabs
title: Views and grids
text: bare list--IDs form splits space evenly opens first tab. non-default ratios non-default open tab, use typed helpers: dock_grid(..., sizes = c(...)) — sizes parallel root children. relative; need sum 1 (normalised ). group(..., sizes = c(...)) — , nested branch root. panels(..., active = "...") — tabbed leaf explicit open tab.

text: group() inner list(...) sizes — use ratios non-root branch: Outer sizes = c(0.3, 0.7) controls root split; inner group(..., sizes = c(0.6, 0.4)) controls right column’s stack.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Nicolas Bennett. Author, maintainer. David Granjon. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Bennett N, Granjon D (2026). blockr.dock: Docking Layout Manager 'blockr'. R package version 0.1.3, https://bristolmyerssquibb.github.io/blockr.dock/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {blockr.dock: A Docking Layout Manager for 'blockr'},   author = {Nicolas Bennett and David Granjon},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.3},   url = {https://bristolmyerssquibb.github.io/blockr.dock/}, }

what: A Docking Layout Manager for blockr
title: A Docking Layout Manager for blockr
text: docking layout manager provided dockViewR can used front-end blockr board using package.

title: A Docking Layout Manager for blockr
text: can install development version blockr.dock GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("BristolMyersSquibb/blockr.dock")

title: A Docking Layout Manager for blockr
text: start board visualizing Sepal.Length Sepal.Width iris dataset: Simple dock
code: library(blockr.dock) library(blockr.core)  serve(   new_dock_board(     blocks = c(       a = new_dataset_block("iris"),       b = new_scatter_block(x = "Sepal.Length", y = "Sepal.Width")     ),     links = list(from = "a", to = "b", input = "data"),     extensions = list(edit = new_edit_board_extension()),     layouts = list("edit", list("a", "b"))   ) )

title: A Docking Layout Manager for blockr
text: locked dock prevents users adding removing blocks extensions. Drag--drop panel resizing also disabled. Locked dock
code: library(blockr.dock) library(blockr.core)  options(blockr.dock_is_locked = TRUE)  serve(   new_dock_board(     blocks = c(       a = new_dataset_block("iris"),       b = new_head_block(n = 20L),       c = new_scatter_block(x = "Sepal.Length", y = "Sepal.Width"),       d = new_subset_block()     ),     links = c(       new_link("a", "b", input = "data"),       new_link("b", "c", input = "data"),       new_link("b", "d", input = "data")     ),     layouts = list(list("a", "b"), list("c", "d"))   ) )

title: A Docking Layout Manager for blockr
text: Since blockr.dock 0.1.1 every board carries dock_layouts object: list one views, exposed tab dropdown top app. single-page case (rendered -tab dock 0.1.0) now dock_layouts one auto-named "Page" view, see Simple dock screenshot . define multiple views explicitly, pass named list layouts. named entry becomes tab; blocks extensions shared across views via board’s DAG, view membership layout concern . Multi-view dock full set shapes accepted new_dock_board(layouts = ...) (nested grids, tabbed panels, active-view selection, coercion rules normalise everything dock_layouts), see vignette("layouts", package = "blockr.dock").
code: library(blockr.core) library(blockr.dock)  board <- new_dock_board(   extensions = blockr.dag::new_dag_extension(),   blocks = c(     dataset_1 = new_dataset_block(),     head_1 = new_head_block()   ),   links = new_link("dataset_1", "head_1"),   layouts = list(     Analysis = dock_layout(       "dag_extension",       panels("dataset_1", "head_1", active = "head_1"),       sizes = c(0.3, 0.7)     ),     Overview = dock_layout("dag_extension"),     Empty = list()   ),   active = "Overview" )  serve(board, "my_board")

title: Board actions — new_action
text: Logic including modal-based UI board actions "append block" "edit stack" can specified using action objects, essentially classed shiny server functions.

title: Board actions — new_action
code: new_action(func, id)  is_action(x)  is_action_generator(x)  action_id(x)  board_actions(x, ...)  action_triggers(x)  block_input_select(   block = NULL,   block_id = NULL,   links = NULL,   mode = c("create", "update", "inputs"),   ... )  block_registry_selectize(id, blocks = list_blocks())  board_select(id, blocks, selected = NULL, ...)

title: Board actions — new_action
text: func function used create shiny::moduleServer(). id Input ID x Object ... Forwarded methods block Block object block_id Block ID links Links object mode Switch determining return object blocks Character vector block registry IDs selected Character vector pre-selected block (registry) IDs

title: Board actions — new_action
text: constructor new_action returns classed function inherits action. Inheritance can checked functions is_action(), is_action_generator() checks whether objects function returns action object. String-value action IDs can retrieved action_id() set actions associated board can enumerated via board_actions(). Finally, action_triggers() returns named list objects suitable use action triggers. utilities block_input_select(), block_registry_selectize() board_select, see respective sections.

title: Board actions — new_action
text: action function can called arguments input, output session, behaving one expect shiny server module function. Actions typically created action generator functions, unique ID shiny::reactiveVal()-based trigger object (inheriting action_trigger). Action trigger objects implement counter-based invalidation mechanism (top reactive values behave).

title: Dock board — new_dock_board
text: Using docking layout manager provided dockViewR, dock_board extends blockr.core::new_board(). addition attributes contained core board, also includes dock extensions (extensions) per-view layout, stored two independent slots – view structure (board_views()) grid geometry (board_grids()). Single-page boards degenerate case one auto-named "Page" view.

title: Dock board — new_dock_board
code: new_dock_board(   blocks = list(),   links = list(),   stacks = list(),   ...,   extensions = new_dock_extensions(),   views = NULL,   grids = NULL,   active = NULL,   options = dock_board_options(),   ctor = NULL,   pkg = NULL,   class = character() )  is_dock_board(x)  as_dock_board(x, ...)  dock_extensions(x)  dock_extensions(x) <- value  dock_ext_ids(x)  extension_ids(x, class = NULL)  dock_board_options()

title: Dock board — new_dock_board
text: blocks Set blocks links Set links stacks Set stacks ... (metadata) attributes extensions Dock extensions. keyed list name, class stripped _extension suffix unnamed (new_dag_extension() becomes dag); key names extension's panel ext() resolves . views Per-view membership: named list keyed view id (minted absent), value dock_view(), member panels named blk() / ext() references bare ids (vector list). NULL yields single default view board's blocks extensions. grids Per-view geometry: named list keyed view id whose values dock_grid()s, dock_grids. Optional – view grid entry falls back default grid members. active Id initially active view (key views). Defaults first view. view active property collection, individual view. options Board-level user settings ctor, pkg Constructor information (used serialization) class Optional extension subclass; supplied, ids extensions inheriting returned. x Board object value Replacement value

title: ID utilities — dock_id
text: Objects, blocks dock extensions carry IDs. can converted ID types, panel IDs "handle" IDs. Panel IDs used refer dock panels, handle IDs provide "handles" DOM manipulations. IDs inherit dock_id panel IDs additionally inherit dock_panel_id, handle IDs inherit dock_handle_id. panel IDs, depending whether panel showing block extension, inheritance structure additionally contains block_panel_id ext_panel_id, respectively. Similarly, handle IDs, block_handle_id ext_handle_id block / extension cards, plus view_handle_id view's DOM container. dock_id objects can converted back native IDs, calling as_obj_id(). utility function dock_id() returns (possibly namespaced) ID dock instance used manage visible panels.

Finally, as_obj_id() returns character vector, dock_id().

title: ID utilities — dock_id
code: blks <- c(   a = blockr.core::new_dataset_block(),   b = blockr.core::new_head_block() )  ext <- new_edit_board_extension()  as_dock_panel_id(blks) #> [1] "block_panel-a" "block_panel-b" #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "block_panel_id" "dock_panel_id"  "dock_id"        as_dock_panel_id(ext) #> [1] "ext_panel-edit_board" #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "ext_panel_id"  "dock_panel_id" "dock_id"        identical(names(blks), as_obj_id(as_block_panel_id(blks))) #> [1] TRUE  as_dock_handle_id(blks) #> [1] "block_handle-a" "block_handle-b" #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "block_handle_id" "dock_handle_id"  "dock_id"         as_dock_handle_id(ext) #> [1] "ext_handle-edit_board" #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "ext_handle_id"  "dock_handle_id" "dock_id"         identical(names(blks), as_obj_id(as_block_handle_id(blks))) #> [1] TRUE

title: Dock grid authoring — dock_grid
text: dock_grid view's geometry: arrangement panels nested splits tab groups, sizes. dock_grid() authoring DSL builds one; view membership (panels belong) display name live view (see dock_view()), grid.

title: Dock grid authoring — dock_grid
code: dock_grid(..., orientation = c("horizontal", "vertical"), sizes = NULL)  panels(..., active = NULL)  group(..., sizes = NULL)  default_layout(blocks, extensions)

title: Dock grid authoring — dock_grid
text: ... dock_grid() group(), grid children (blk() / ext() references, bare ids, character vectors, lists, panels(), group()). panels(), panel references ids. reference used carry placement hint. Otherwise reserved generic consistency. orientation Top-level split direction; one "horizontal" (default) "vertical". sizes Numeric vector parallel ..., giving child's share parent (positive; need sum 1). active panels(), id tab open default. blocks, extensions Dock board components arrange (default_layout()).

title: Dock grid authoring — dock_grid
text: dock_grid() returns dock_grid object. panels() returns dock_panels node group() returns dock_group node – grid sub-trees usable inside dock_grid() / group(). default_layout() returns list views (dock_views) grids (dock_grids).

title: Dock grid authoring — dock_grid
text: Construct grid : dock_grid(...): page-level container. ... children root branch. Bare strings become single-panel leaves, character vectors become tabbed leaves, lists become nested branches. Use panels() tabbed leaf explicit open tab, group() branch explicit sizes. panels(..., active = NULL): tabbed leaf whose tab strip holds given panel ids. active selects initially-open tab; first id wins default. single-panel panels() permitted redundant (bare string equivalent). group(..., sizes = NULL): branch container. sizes numeric vector parallel ... overrides even split. default_layout(blocks, extensions) produces default board arrangement (extensions top, blocks ) list(views, grids) constructor consumes. dock_grid() accepts orientation = "horizontal" | "vertical" top-level split direction sizes root-branch ratios. dockView-native {grid, panels, activeGroup} payload dockViewR consumes dock_layout, built grid board's blocks extensions.

title: Dock grid authoring — dock_grid
code: blks <- c(   a = blockr.core::new_dataset_block(),   b = blockr.core::new_head_block() )  # Panels named with typed references; bare id strings work too panels(blk("a"), blk("b"), active = blk("b")) #> $views #> $views[[1]] #> [1] "block_panel-a" #>  #> $views[[2]] #> [1] "block_panel-b" #>  #>  #> $active #> [1] "block_panel-b" #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "dock_panels" "dock_node"    # Branch with explicit child ratios group(blk("a"), blk("b"), sizes = c(0.3, 0.7)) #> $children #> $children[[1]] #> <panel_ref> block_panel-a  #>  #> $children[[2]] #> <panel_ref> block_panel-b  #>  #>  #> $sizes #> [1] 0.3 0.7 #>  #> attr(,"class") #> [1] "dock_group" "dock_node"   # An extension panel beside a tabbed block leaf dock_grid(   ext("dag"),   panels(blk("a"), blk("b"), active = blk("b")),   sizes = c(0.3, 0.7) ) #> <dock_grid> horizontal #> ├─ dag (30%) #> └─ tabs (70%) #>    ├─ a #>    └─ b (active)  # Vertical top-level split dock_grid(blk("a"), blk("b"), orientation = "vertical") #> <dock_grid> vertical #> ├─ a (50%) #> └─ b (50%)

title: Get block metadata — blks_metadata
text: Returns various metadata blocks block categories, well styling block icons.

title: Get block metadata — blks_metadata
code: blks_metadata(blocks)  blk_color(category)  blk_icon_data_uri(icon_svg, color, size = 48, mode = c("uri", "inline"))  block_status_badge(status, error_count = 0L)

title: Get block metadata — blks_metadata
text: blocks Blocks passed blocks block object category Block category icon_svg Character string containing SVG icon markup color Hex color code background size Numeric size pixels (default: 48) mode Switch URI inline HTML mode status block eval status. waiting, unset failed carry badge; ready carries none; dormant indeterminate; value yields badge. size coloured dot's pixel diameter ring white outline width, shared dock card icon DAG node badge render identically. error_count Number error conditions block raised. positive count promotes badge failed, catching render-phase errors leave eval status ready.

title: Get block metadata — blks_metadata
text: Metadata returned blks_metadata() data.frame row corresponding block. blk_color() blk_icon_data_uri() return character vectors. block_status_badge() returns list color, label, size, ring ring_color (badge draw), NULL status badge, NA status indeterminate.

title: Get block metadata — blks_metadata
text: blks_metadata(): Retrieves metadata given block blocks object block registry. Can also handle blocks registered provides default values case. blk_color(): Produces colors using Okabe-Ito colorblind-friendly palette character vector block categories. blk_icon_data_uri(): Processes block icons add color turn square-shaped icons. block_status_badge(): Derives block's status badge eval status error count – single derivation dock card icon blockr.dag node badge share, always render colour styling. Returns styling list (draw badge), NULL (badge), NA (indeterminate: status computed, leave existing badge unchanged).

title: Typed panel references — blk
text: id block extension id (wire-prefixed panel id). near ref bare id anchor placement . side Placement direction relative near: one within, left, right, , . size Target size ratio (0, 1) – consumed resize, recorded add set_size floor lands (#320). x object. ... Ignored.

title: Typed panel references — blk
text: blk() / ext() return panel_ref. .character() one returns canonical panel id, is_panel_ref() returns boolean.

title: Typed panel references — blk
text: ref optionally carries placement hint, verb's operands unnamed list self-describing refs: add = list(blk("", near = "b", side = "right"), ext("dag")). hint fields meaningful depends verb – add consumes near / side / size, move consumes near / side – hint ref used placement happens (rm, select, near anchor, dock_grid() / panels() authoring DSL) loud error. hints constructor arguments, misspelled one (blk("", sise = 0.4)) fails call site R's unused-argument error, payload exists. Bare id strings accepted sugar wherever ref , resolved block-first hard error true cross-namespace clash (id block extension), demands typed ref.

Inheritance can checked using is_dock_stack(), returns scalar logical color attribute can set retrieved using stack_color<-() (returns modified stack object invisibly) stack_color() (returns string), respectively. Stack objects may coerced "dock_stack" using as_dock_stack() finally, utility function suggest_new_colors() returns character vector new colors, based existing palette.

what: Dock views: structure and grid — is_dock_grids
title: Dock views: structure and grid — is_dock_grids
text: dock_board stores views two independent slots. Structure – panels belong view, plus view names, ids active view – dock_views collection dock_view objects, read board_views(). Grid – geometry view (nesting, tab groups, sizes) – separate, NULL-valid dock_grids slot, read board_grids(). Single-page boards degenerate case: one auto-named "Page" view. Blocks extensions shared across views via board's DAG; view membership layout concern .

title: Dock views: structure and grid — is_dock_grids
code: is_dock_grids(x)  validate_dock_grids(x, views = NULL)  board_grids(x)  board_grids(x) <- value  as_dock_view(x, ...)  dock_view(members = character(), name = NULL)  is_dock_view(x)  validate_dock_view(x)  view_members(x)  is_dock_views(x)  validate_dock_views(x)  view_name(x)  view_name(x) <- value  view_names(x)  active_view(x)  active_view(x) <- value  board_views(x)  board_views(x) <- value

title: Dock views: structure and grid — is_dock_grids
text: x object appropriate function: dock_view (view_name(), view_members()), dock_views collection (view_names(), active_view()), dock_grids, dock_board (board accessors). views dock_views collection, used check grids key known views. value Replacement value ... Forwarded methods. members Ordered character vector panel ids. name Optional display name view.

title: Dock views: structure and grid — is_dock_grids
text: board_views() returns dock_views, board_grids() dock_grids NULL, setters modified board invisibly. dock_view() returns dock_view view_members() character vector. is_dock_view() / is_dock_views() / is_dock_grids() return boolean; validate_dock_view(), validate_dock_views() validate_dock_grids() return (validated) input throw error. active_view() returns active view's id, NULL view active, active_view<-() modified collection (dock_board) invisibly. view_name() returns view's explicit display name (NULL), view_name<-() modified view, view_names() character vector display labels keyed view id (derived id view explicit name). as_dock_view() returns dock_view: identity dock_view, view whose members dock_layout's panel ids.

title: Dock views: structure and grid — is_dock_grids
text: view carries stable, immutable id (key collection) distinct editable display name. mirrors id / name separation used blocks (immutable id keys collection; blockr.core::block_name() editable label). id minted view created never changes – rename rewrites name attribute, never key. Use dock_view() construct view, view_name() / view_name<-() read write display name, view_names() names collection, view_members() dock_view's ordered panel-id set. Multi-view boards defined passing views (optionally grids) new_dock_board(): views named list keyed view id (minted absent), value dock_view(), bare character vector member panel ids, list panel ids. grids named list keyed view id whose values dock_grid()s; optional – view grid entry falls back default grid members wherever placement geometry needed. Bare block / extension ids either slot resolved canonical panel ids board's blocks extensions. initially-active view chosen new_dock_board(active = ) (view id), defaulting first; property collection, never individual view. View CRUD enabled unless dock locked (see is_dock_locked()). Structure grid related total semantics, containment: member grid entry un-landed intent, grid entry membership inert ghost. legal committed board reconciled placement read (view_grid() prunes ghosts shows un-landed members via default) – board valid grid . Referential integrity still holds: every member must reference block extension board.

title: Dock views: structure and grid — is_dock_grids
code: brd <- new_dock_board(   blocks = c(     dataset_1 = blockr.core::new_dataset_block(),     head_1 = blockr.core::new_head_block()   ),   views = list(     analysis = dock_view(c("dataset_1", "head_1"), name = "Analysis"),     overview = "dataset_1"   ),   active = "overview" ) view_names(board_views(brd)) #>   analysis   overview  #> "Analysis" "Overview"  view_members(board_views(brd)[["analysis"]]) #> [1] "block_panel-dataset_1" "block_panel-head_1"

title: blockr.dock (development version)
text: startup board now builds active view’s block cards; -screen views’ cards built first visit rather front (#272). board_ui() rendered edit card every block across every view static offcanvas mount, first paint scaled total block count, screen (~20s renderTags 99-block, 12-view board). now renders active view’s cards defers rest; switch_active_view() (active-dock panel-op path) inserts view’s cards first time shown, core’s required channel doubles build ledger revisit never doubles card. Block visibility coordinated blockr.core (>= 0.1.4) two-channel visibility interface: per-block required channel dock drives (TRUE screen, FALSE built screen) visible channel writes view id client painted view. gives blockr.core explicit “initial view painted” signal gate background block-server construction , instead inferring readiness result-quiescence.

title: blockr.dock 0.1.2
text: CRAN release: 2026-07-13 block-browser, link-menu, stack-menu sidebar UI components now bundled directly blockr.dock instead imported (non-CRAN) blockr.ui package, blockr.dock installs CRAN remote dependencies. Panel operations now first-class verbs views$mod update payload (add / rm / move / select), panels named new typed references blk() / ext() instead wire-id prefixes. Breaking: old set-replace membership form retired. Breaking: extension’s id now owned container (mirroring blocks), serving single identity everywhere – wire panel id, DOM handle, module namespace ext() target. Breaking (extension authors): extension’s live result now reaches actions, callbacks peer extensions extensions bundle keyed extension id; new exported extension_ids() resolves class runtime id(s) container assigned. dock extension opens block’s panel views grammar (compose active + select) rather retired live dock handle; exported show_panel() removed. block status badge now one exported helper, block_status_badge(), shared blockr.dag dock card icon DAG node badge render identically; dock dot now also reflects render-phase errors. Renaming block longer crashes board block absent view – placed one view, parked offcanvas panel. board’s per-view layout splits two independent slots: server-authoritative dock_views structure collection (read board_views()) separate NULL-valid dock_grids geometry slot (board_grids()), grid validated subset view’s membership. Block eval status now first-class panel affordance: waiting unset block shows dedicated placeholder instead generic warning, colour-coded dot card header marks waiting, unset failed blocks glance. Per-block condition UI (warnings, messages, errors) updated surgically stable condition id, persistent warning longer flashes every re-evaluation. busy pulse longer flashes plain panel switch: CSS rule gates genuinely recomputing output, startup block evaluation still pulse bare navigation . “Edit board” extension longer churns board re-emit – re-syncs staged working copy links stacks actually change, stops flickering manage-links cell inputs, overlays half-finished staged edits instead clobbering . Dock extensions now receive view_data, live -views layout reactive serialization also reads (NULL every view reported , req() ); active-view dock handle retired extension surface. Multi-view boards longer emit burst redundant board updates startup; fold mirrored every dockview focus tick back board_layouts removed, live layout read demand instead. dock longer loops tears panels slow client: view’s arrangement now client-owned flows dock -> board , removing reconcile push whose echo restore impoverished layout. Live panel rearrangements longer lost save – view_data(), live layout serialization reads, longer stays stuck NULL whole session. Adding block dock view finished initialising longer throws argument length zero. block, link stack action handlers now mount corresponding blockr.ui menu modules (card-list pickers search inline editing), replacing every per-field Shiny input; link menu adds bidirectional source / target picking stack menu inline colour picker. block browsers pre-rendered toggled rather rebuilt open, dock-side *_sidebar_body() bodies spec helpers removed. Layout deserialization now routes producing blockr.dock version (read constructor$version) rather sniffing payload shape, keeping shape discrimination fallback version-less payloads. dock “manager” object gone: apply_board_update.dock_board() pure reducer live view surgery runs one reconcile pass driven committed board. augment_board_update.dock_board() now idempotent, fixing view-add loop. Views now carry stable, immutable id decoupled editable display name (mirroring blocks): dock_layouts keyed id, name read written via view_name() / view_name<-(), views delta gains rename slot. new_dock_board(layouts = list(...)) list name view’s id, producers addressed views name (e.g. blockr.assistant) must switch ids. dock_layout now format() / print() methods render arrangement indented tree; panel ids print without wire prefixes unless bare = FALSE. views slot board_update payload structured delta (add / mod / rm / active) instead wholesale dock_layouts replacement, composing atomically blocks / links / stacks. UI-driven layout changes (panel close / add, drag-resize, view CRUD) route lifecycle debounced (250 ms), removing block drops panel surgically rather clearing active view. Requires blockr.core (>= 0.1.3).

title: blockr.dock 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-29 Added prepend block action. Define multi-view boards passing named list new_dock_board(layouts = ...): Mark arrangement initially active dock_layout(..., active = TRUE): none marked, first one used. board’s initialise_layout normalises slot dock_layout (storing arrangement ), stores result dock_layouts collection. Breaking changes layout API: Renamed new_dock_board()’s layout parameter layouts (corresponding board field), since boards now hold dock_layouts collection. Renamed board accessor dock_layout(brd) (setter dock_layout(brd) <-) active_layout(brd) / active_layout(brd) <-. Renamed board_views(brd) board_layouts(brd), corresponding setter dock_layouts(brd) <- value board_layouts(brd) <- value. dock_layout now per-view arrangement type. dock_layout(...) constructs one nested list block / extension IDs accepts orientation, sizes, active arguments. previously-exported “fully-resolved” dock_layout (grid + panels wire shape) gone — panel content derived board’s blocks extensions demand dockview boundary, per-view storage shrinks just arrangement panel definitions longer duplicate across views. Added panels(..., active = NULL) tabbed leaves explicit open tab, group(..., sizes = NULL) nested branches explicit ratios. dock_layout() also accepts sizes = root-level ratios orientation = top-level split direction. Removed dock_view(), dock_grid(), is_dock_grid(), as_dock_grid(). Use dock_layout(...) (new panels() / group()) per-view spec. Renamed default_layout() → kept name default_layout(). now returns dock_layout (arrangement ) — previously-exported default_grid() (panel-ID form) gone. Unexported high-level resolver create_dock_layout() (renamed internally resolve_dock_layout()). Unexported dock_layouts() constructor. user-facing input shape new_dock_board(layouts = ...) plain named list — dock_layouts type resolved collection board holds internally. is_dock_layouts(), as_dock_layouts(), validate_dock_layouts() remain exported. Unexported new_dock_layout(); use dock_layout() instead. Unexported view_ids() view_can_crud(). internal helpers exposed accident; renamed layout_ids() views_can_crud() respectively align operate . Wire format serialised dock_layout decoupled dockview’s internal tree. persisted shape flattened recursive spec: top object carries orientation, children, optional sizes, optional focus (panel current focus); child bare string (single-panel leaf), {panels, active?} object (tabbed leaf), {children, sizes?} object (nested branch). Sizes ratios (auto-normalised dockview’s pixel sizes); even splits omit sizes; default open tab omits active; focus first leaf omits focus. focus round-trips focused group (dockview’s activeGroup) naming stable panel rather regenerated group id. Legacy payloads (dockview-shape grid field) load via shape-discriminated reader. Producer-version routing tracked #153 (depends blockr.core forwarding ... blockr_deser.list). Layout conversion API split boundary. R object ↔︎ R list boundary uses coercion: as_dock_layout() coerces dock_layout (identity), board (active layout), spec list dock_layout; .list() dock_layout returns spec list. R object ↔︎ JSON string boundary uses explicit verbs: layout_to_json() / layout_from_json(). as_dock_layout(<list>) layout_from_json() take optional blocks / extensions resolve bare IDs validate. layout_panel_ids() / panel_obj_ids() inspect panel / object IDs layout references. dockview wire format converters part public API — dock_layout object, JSON, spec list ; as_dock_layout() rejects dockview grid-shaped list.
code: layouts = list(   Analysis = list("block_1", "block_2"),   Overview = list("dag_extension") ) layouts = list(   Analysis = list("block_1", "block_2"),   Overview = dock_layout("dag_extension", active = TRUE) )

title: blockr.dock 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-12-11 Initial CRAN submission
